perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms of this Agreement (the "Term").
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be determined by mutual agreement of both parties, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms of this Agreement (the "Term").
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific duration of one year and replacing it with 'mutual agreement', the contract creates uncertainty regarding the agreement's lifespan. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because several sections, such as sections 9 and 10, refer to actions taken 'on the Closing Date,' implying a defined term related to that date, which is contradicted by this vague agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HPIL ET shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless GINARES from, against and with respect to any claim, liability, obligations, loss, damage, assessment, judgment, cost and expense (including, without limitation, reasonable attorneys' and accountants' fees and costs and expenses reasonably incurred in investigating, preparing, defending against or prosecuting any litigation or claim, action, suit, proceeding or demand) or any kind or character (collectively, "Losses") arising out of or in any manner, incident, relating or attributable to: any inaccuracy in any representation or breach of warranty of GINARES contained in this Agreement and (ii) any failure by HPIL ET to perform or observe, or to have performed or observed in full any covenant, agreement or condition to be performed or observed by HPIL ET under this Agreement or any of the other agreements or instruments executed and delivered by HPIL ET on the Closing Date.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a term of one year. The changed text removes this fixed term and replaces it with a term 'determined by mutual agreement'. This change creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'the Closing Date' and actions taken on that date. The 'Closing Date' implies a defined term length, which is contradicted by the 'mutual agreement' term introduced in the changed text. This uncertainty regarding the agreement's term duration directly conflicts with provisions tied to the 'Closing Date', causing an in-text contradiction as defined under Omissions – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties are working cooperatively to develop and cooperate to expand the GINARES projects. The Parties agree to develop a list of target cooperation projects and common goals, and consequent agreements if required, within six (6) Months of signing this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties are working cooperatively to develop and cooperate to expand the GINARES projects. The Parties agree to develop a list of target cooperation projects and common goals, and consequent agreements if required.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the 'within six (6) Months of signing this Agreement' clause eliminates a key deadline for establishing target projects. This causes a contradiction with Section 3, which outlines specific obligations of both parties, implying a structured cooperative effort that now lacks a defined timeframe for initial project planning. The 'as appropriate' clauses in Section 3 now become significantly more ambiguous without a specific initiation timeline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Obligations Of The Parties 3.1. HPIL ET's obligations under this Agreement are to: (i) Follow up on developments regarding GINARES; (ii) Work with GINARES as appropriate. 3.2. GINARES's obligations under this Agreement are to: (i) Keep HPIL ET aware of developments regarding GINARES; (ii) Work with HPIL ET as appropriate.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the 'within six (6) Months of signing this Agreement' clause creates a contradiction. Section 3 outlines obligations of both parties to 'work with GINARES as appropriate'. Without the defined timeframe, the 'as appropriate' clause becomes too vague, potentially undermining the original intent of a structured cooperative effort outlined in Section 3. The removed timeline directly impacts the enforceability and clarity of Section 3, creating a strong contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All instruments or documents to be delivered by any Party to this Agreement shall be in form and content reasonably satisfactory to the counsel for the Party receiving such instrument or document.
perturbation.changed_text: All instruments or documents to be delivered by any Party to this Agreement shall be delivered in a timely manner.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for 'form and content reasonably satisfactory to the counsel,' the contract eliminates a crucial safeguard ensuring legal review and approval of delivered documents. This contradicts the spirit of Section 12, which implies that both parties have the right to legal counsel's assessment of instruments related to the agreement. Moreover, it contradicts Section 7 and 8 (GINARES and HPIL ET's Representations and Warranties) where it ensures that the agreement is legally binding and authorised, and removing this clause, can make the agreement less legally secure, which may undermine the parties' ability to ensure its authorization.
perturbation.contradicted_text: GINARES hereby represents, warrants, and covenants to HPIL ET that: 7.1. Authorization This Agreement constitutes a valid and legally binding obligation of GINARES, enforceable in accordance with its terms, except (i) as limited by applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium and other laws of general application affecting enforcement of creditors' rights generally and (ii) as limited by laws relating to the availability of specific performance, injunctive relief or other equitable remedies.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires documents to be 'reasonably satisfactory to counsel,' ensuring legal review. The changed text eliminates this requirement, only mandating timely delivery. This contradicts Section 7.1, which warrants the agreement's legally binding nature. By removing the counsel's review, the agreement's authorization and enforceability, as guaranteed in Section 7.1, are undermined, creating a direct conflict. The original text allows GINARES to ensure authorization of the agreement, and by removing this safeguard, it makes Section 7.1's warranty more difficult to fulfill.
